title: Mom's Train Table
summary: A low kids' activity table with a fixed plywood box top, a removable bottom shelf, and a trimmed-out top surface split into liftable inserts for easy cleanup and storage access.
description:
Squaring the box is the one step worth doing carefully and by feel rather than by a single measurement: measure both diagonals, and whichever one is longer, gently push those two corners toward each other until the diagonals match — that's the whole technique, and it works on any rectangular frame, not just this one. Every later step depends on this box actually being square, since the top trim gets fitted around the tabletop inserts afterward, and an out-of-square box makes for an ill-fitting insert no amount of trimming fixes cleanly.

The top surface is intentionally NOT one solid panel — it's trimmed out around removable plywood inserts, sanded to a snug but liftable fit, specifically so a child (or parent) can lift sections out to access the shelf storage underneath, or to swap in a section with train tracks already set up. A small finger-hole drilled in the center of each insert is what makes lifting them back out easy without needing a pry tool.

materials.name: Plywood or MDF, 3/4", ripped into three 31-7/8" x 48" strips
materials.note: One strip becomes the fixed bottom; the others become the top surface and its removable inserts.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Screw the box sides together with 2" screws and glue. Measure both diagonals — one will be longer. Gently push those two opposite corners together to shorten that diagonal until both measurements match; that's how you square up any rectangular frame.

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Lay one of the plywood or MDF strips on top of the box and screw down with 1-1/4" screws. Predrill every hole and use glue.
materials: Plywood or MDF, 3/4", ripped into three 31-7/8" x 48" strips

steps.title: Attach the end legs
steps.body: Screw the side legs to the short ends of the box with 1-1/4" screws and glue, keeping them flush to the sides and top.

steps.title: Attach the side legs
steps.body: Screw the front/back legs to the end legs and the box sides with 1-1/4" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: With help, or with the table turned on its side, attach the bottom shelf supports and shelf. Cutting small spacer blocks and screwing them to the inside of the legs first, flush with the leg bottoms, gives the shelf something to rest on while you predrill and screw it down — the spacers can stay in place afterward.

steps.title: Add the side and end trim
steps.body: Attach the side and end trim pieces to the box with nails and glue, or screws if you don't have a nail gun.

steps.title: Add the top trim
steps.body: Lay out the tabletop insert pieces on top of the box and trim around them so they have a snug but removable fit — the top trim attaches to the legs and side trim, not directly to the box top.

steps.title: Fit the top inserts
steps.body: Sand the tabletop inserts to get a nice snug fit within the trim. Drill a small finger-hole in the center of each insert so they can be lifted back out easily. Fill nail holes, sand every surface, and finish.
materials: Plywood or MDF, 3/4", ripped into three 31-7/8" x 48" strips

images.alt: Kids' train table with a trimmed top and removable inserts
